skill.name: backend-test-writer
skill.description: Generates backend tests for MERN stack projects by inspecting Express routes, controllers, services, models, middleware, and utilities.
skill.summary: Generates Jest, Supertest, and MongoDB test plans for backend code.
skill.author: 9tykeshav

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, environment, cryptography, and combination findings. Review found these are Markdown test examples and setup guidance, not executable skill code or prompt injection. The remaining risk is low because users may follow dependency install commands and generated tests may use environment secrets during local test setup.

low_findings.title: Dependency and Test Commands in Documentation
low_findings.description: The skill tells users to run npm test and provides an npm install command for test dependencies. This is expected for a backend testing skill, but users should review project scripts and packages before executing them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines explicitly show local test execution and dependency installation guidance. The commands are ordinary testing workflow commands, with no evidence of malicious arguments or remote exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Environment Secret Example in Test Token Generation
low_findings.description: The auth middleware example signs a JWT with process.env.JWT_SECRET. This is normal in tests, but users should use isolated test secrets instead of production secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: There is direct evidence of environment variable access in a Markdown test example. The surrounding context is local auth middleware testing, so the concern is limited to safe test configuration.

low_findings.title: Static Findings Are Markdown Example False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command, network, filesystem, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and combination alerts are caused by fenced code examples, inline code ticks, require paths, Supertest requests, mocks, and sample passwords. No executable skill script or prompt injection text was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious strings occur inside Markdown instructions and JavaScript examples rather than active code. Manual review found no file that executes these snippets automatically.

content.user_title: Generate Backend Tests for MERN Apps
content.value_statement: Backend teams often miss route, service, model, and middleware edge cases. This skill creates practical Jest, Supertest, and MongoDB test coverage from existing project structure.
actual_capabilities: Detects Jest, Vitest, or Mocha setup from package.json before writing tests.
Maps backend file types to route, controller, service, model, middleware, or utility test strategies.
Generates success, validation, not found, auth failure, and edge case test coverage.
Provides setup patterns for Jest, Supertest, MongoDB memory server, fixtures, and auth helpers.
Recommends integration tests for routes and unit tests for isolated services and utilities.
limitations: Focused on JavaScript MERN stack backend projects, not general backend frameworks.
Requires reading the target code before reliable tests can be generated.
Does not guarantee tests pass without adapting imports, factories, and app bootstrapping.
May require users to install testing dependencies in their local project.

use_cases.title: Add API Regression Coverage
use_cases.description: Generate Supertest coverage for Express routes, including validation failures, duplicate records, and auth behavior.

use_cases.title: Prepare Pull Requests Faster
use_cases.description: Create targeted Jest tests for changed services, models, middleware, and utilities before requesting review.

use_cases.title: Standardize Test Setup
use_cases.description: Introduce consistent setup files, fixtures, cleanup patterns, and helper structure across a MERN backend.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Tests for One File
prompt_templates.prompt: Use backend-test-writer on this Express route file. Check the test setup first, then generate route tests for success, validation, not found, and auth cases.

prompt_templates.title: Review Existing Test Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this service and its existing tests. Identify coverage gaps, then add focused unit tests for important branches and edge cases.

prompt_templates.title: Create Integration Test Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up backend integration testing for this MERN API. Use Jest, Supertest, MongoDB memory server, cleanup hooks, fixtures, and auth helpers.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Full Backend Test Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these routes, services, models, and middleware. Prioritize P0 and P1 cases, generate tests sequentially, and summarize what remains untested.

output_examples.input: A user route with create, read, update, delete, duplicate email checks, and protected profile access.
output: A route integration test plan covering success responses, invalid input, duplicate records, missing records, and unauthorized access.
Setup notes for Supertest and isolated database cleanup between tests.
A short summary of generated test files and cases.

output_examples.input: A user service with repository and email dependencies.
output: A unit test plan using mocked repository and email dependencies.
Coverage for creation, duplicate detection, dependency failure, and expected return values.

output_examples.input: A project without a configured backend test framework.
output: An infrastructure checklist for selecting and configuring Jest with backend test helpers.
A dependency list and setup sequence for local review before installation.

best_practices: Start with package.json and existing tests before generating new files.
Use integration tests for routes and unit tests for services, utilities, models, and middleware.
Use isolated test secrets, test fixtures, and database cleanup for every test run.
anti_patterns: Do not generate tests for code that has not been read and understood.
Do not test only the happy path while skipping validation and auth failures.
Do not rely on production databases, production secrets, or live external APIs in tests.

faq.question: Which backend stack does this skill support best?
faq.answer: It is designed for MERN backends using Express, Node.js, MongoDB, Mongoose, Jest, and Supertest.

faq.question: Can it work with Vitest or Mocha?
faq.answer: It checks for Jest, Vitest, or Mocha, but the reference patterns are strongest for Jest.

faq.question: Does it install dependencies automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can suggest dependency commands, but users should review and run them manually.

faq.question: Does it require a real MongoDB database?
faq.answer: No. The setup guidance uses MongoDB memory server for isolated integration tests.

faq.question: Can it add tests to existing projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It first checks existing test conventions and then writes tests that match the project structure.

faq.question: Will generated tests always pass immediately?
faq.answer: No. Imports, app setup, factories, and auth helpers may need project-specific adjustments.
